LAW AND MOTION, 301, MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND, SUBMITTED ON JAN-11-2005 BY JAMES L. WARREN. DEFENDANT JANETTA JONES' MOTION IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION TO DETERMINE THE NECESSITY OF A HEARING (PERSONAL SERVICE AS OPPOSED TO PERSONAL NOTICE). JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558

LAW AND MOTION 301, EVIDENTIARY HEARING. JOHN SHEPARD WAS SWORN AND TESTIFIED FOR PLAINTIFF, AND CHARLES E. JONES, SR. WAS SWORN AND TESTIFIED FOR DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF'S EXHIBIT 1 WAS MARKED FOR IDENTIFICATION. DEFENDANT'S EXHIBIT A AND B WAS MARKED AND ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE WAS NO PROPER SERVICE IN THIS CASE. BY STIPULATION OF COUNSEL, JANETTA JONES IS SERVED. ANSWER TO BE FILED WITH THE RULING OF DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT. EXHIBITS WERE RETURNED. JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN, REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558

calendar.matters:
Follow-Up Hearing (In Connection With)
Off Calendar Mar-17-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Steven Booska, Esq., (415) 397-4345, for Plaintiff Comdata Network, Inc.
John H. Aspelin, Esq., Aspelin & Bridgman, (415) 296-9812, for Defendant Janetta Jones
RULING:
Evidentiary hearing. John Shepard was sworn and testified for Plaintiff, and Charles E. Jones, Sr. was sworn and testified for Defendant. Plaintiff's Exhibit 1 was marked for identification. Defendant's Exhibit A and B was marked and admitted into evidence. The Court finds that there was no proper service in this case. By stipulation of counsel, Janetta Jones is served. Answer to be filed with the ruling of Defendant's Motion to set aside default. Exhibits were returned. (301/JLW/ds) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
John Aspelin, Esq., Aspelin & Bridgman LLP (415) 296-9812 for Defendant and Moving Party Comdata Network, Inc.
Steven Booska, Esq. (415) 397-4345 for Plaintiff and Opposing Party Comdata Network, Inc
RULING:
A R G U E D; DEFENDANT JANETTA JONES' MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is taken UNDER SUBMISSION to determine the necessity of a hearing (personal service as opposed to personal notice). (301/JLW/) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
